scope: FQ-13: enemy variety and combat pressure - promote thornrat/ore_tick/raider_torchbearer to live with distinct roles, spawn conditions, damage profiles, and drops, plus the world/entity hooks that make each pressure distinct. No new world-gen, bosses, or NPC labor.
summary: The world feels less empty and the combat and farming systems finally have consumers. FQ-13 promotes three enemies.json stubs to status live, each genuinely distinct rather than a reskinned slime. The thornrat is a fast (speed 66), frail (hp_mult 0.7) surface harasser whose whole point is agriculture: its targets_crops flag makes simple_threat hunt the nearest crop within range and eat it through world.eat_crop, which clears the cell with NO player yield - the lost harvest is the pressure - and it falls back to hall-seeking when no crop is around. It appears at night once past day 2, difficulty-gated, at most one per night. The ore_tick is a frail underground ore-pocket nuisance: _advance_cave_spawns now chooses it over the cave crawler whenever world.has_ore_within finds an ore-family block near the spawn cell, so it reads as an ore-vein dweller. The raider_torchbearer is the base-pressure escalation: tankier (hp_mult 1.5), harder-hitting (contact_damage 10 vs 8), and it burns the Town Hall about 2.5x faster (hall_dps_mult 2.5), joining later raids through its own spawn_rule (day 8 / stockpile 40 gate, low base chance) rolled independently of the basic raider. The plumbing stayed small and data-first: _spawn_enemy_at gained per-def hp_mult, hall_dps_mult, and targets_crops, all defaulting so the original surface_slime/cave_crawler/raider_basic are byte-for-byte unchanged; world.gd added nearest_crop, eat_crop (no-drop crop removal mirroring break_block bookkeeping), has_ore_within, and an ORE_IDS set; items.json added seven drop items for names and icons. Density is deliberately conservative pending human combat tuning. This run also fixed a recurring GDScript inference hang - var target := world.cell_center(crop) can't infer a type because the threat's world is typed Node2D, so simple_threat.gd failed to load and the headless smoke hung in a per-frame error loop; annotating var target: Vector2 restored a clean run. Suite 276 -> 283, and the enemies_json_loads live-def count moved 3 -> 6.
acceptance: All three MVP-expansion enemies are live (fq13_new_enemies_live)
Thornrat crop-eating: nearest_crop locates a crop and eat_crop clears it with zero player food yield (fq13_thornrat_eats_crop)
A spawned thornrat carries targets_crops and its fast profile, speed 66 (fq13_thornrat_profile)
Ore tick keys off ore: has_ore_within true beside a vein, false in scrubbed stone (fq13_ore_tick_near_ore)
Torchbearer burns the hall faster and hits harder than a basic raider: hall_dps 10 vs 4, atk 10 vs 8 (fq13_torchbearer_burns_faster)
Distinct hp profiles via hp_mult: torchbearer hp 5 > thornrat hp 2 (fq13_enemy_hp_profile)
New-enemy drops enter the inventory on death (fq13_new_enemy_drops)
Suite 283/283; validator (FQ-13 enemy variety) and capsule doctor green
validation: python scripts/validate_repo.py -> PASS (exit 0), incl. 'FQ-13 enemy variety'
COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited headless run -> 283/283 PASS, 7 new fq13_* checks green
git diff --check -> 0 whitespace errors (LF->CRLF notices only)
decisions: Promote existing enemies.json stubs to live rather than invent new ids - the MVP expansion order already named thornrat/ore_tick/raider_torchbearer

Keep the plumbing data-first: optional per-def hp_mult/hall_dps_mult/targets_crops on the shared _spawn_enemy_at, defaulting so the original three enemies are unchanged, instead of subclassing simple_threat
Thornrat crop-eating gives the player nothing (world.eat_crop, no drop) - the distinct pressure is the lost harvest, tying FQ-13 to the FQ-12 farming loop
Ore tick is a spawn-selection variant of the underground path (chosen when ore is near) rather than a new spawn system
Torchbearer is rolled independently of the basic raider with a later spawn_rule, so a night can bring either or both; hall_dps_mult expresses 'burns structures' without a fire/DoT system
Conservative density (at most one thornrat/torchbearer per night, day- and difficulty-gated) pending human combat tuning
Self-review only (no agent spawned) for a change fully covered by the validator + 7 smoke checks
risks: Enemy art is the family-tinted drawn-rect fallback; per-enemy sprites are optional assets pending in docs/ASSET_ROADMAP.md (validator INFO, fallbacks active)
Combat/spawn tuning (damage, hp_mult, hall_dps_mult 2.5, spawn chances, day thresholds) is data-tunable and untested by human play

Thornrat only eats crops within CROP_SEEK_CELLS and ignores farm_soil (eats the crop, not the tilled tile); hp remains a shared baseline scaled by hp_mult rather than fully independent per-enemy hp curves
next_action: FQ-14: goal panel, tutorial prompts, and playtest checklist.
